pattern: Bucket characters by their exact occurrence count, then pick the bucket holding the most distinct characters, breaking ties toward the higher count.
hints: First reduce the string to how many times each distinct character appears.
Group characters by their occurrence count and ask which group has the most members.
Among groups tied for the largest membership, prefer the one whose occurrence count is greater, then output that group's characters.

edge_inputs.description: all distinct, one big group at frequency one
two characters tie on group size, higher frequency wins
group-size tie resolved by larger frequency
a larger group at a lower frequency must not win over the tie rule
everything appears the same number of times

stress.description: random short string over a small alphabet so frequency groups overlap and ties occur
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 100)))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abcdef') for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
